source_pr.title: tikv: fix race modification when handle epoch not match
source_pr.body: ### What problem does this PR solve? Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/16961 Problem Summary: after https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/16961 two resolve lock requests maybe share one response, we need to make sure there is no modification for the shared response. (we are hard to deep copy them every time too) ### What is changed and how it works? What's Changed: ShallowCopy metapb.Region struct on epoch not match How it Works: ### Related changes - Need to cherry-pick to the release branch 4.0 ### Check List Tests - Unit test - Integration test Side effects - n/a ### Release note fix race modification when handle epoch not match --- This change is [](https://reviewable.io/reviews/pingcap/tidb/16962)

symptoms: PR body: two resolve-lock requests may share one response; avoid modifying shared response (race).
failure_signature: PR body: two resolve-lock requests may share one response; avoid modifying shared response (race).
root_cause_explanation: Two concurrent resolve-lock requests could share the same PD response (region meta). The code decoded region keys in-place, which would mutate the shared response object and create a race/corruption when another request reads it. The fix introduces a shallow-copy decode path and uses it on RegionEpochNotMatch so the shared response is not modified.
fix_pattern: Avoid mutating shared PD responses: shallow-copy Region meta before decoding StartKey/EndKey when handling RegionEpochNotMatch.

evidence: evidence: PR body: two resolve-lock requests may share one response; avoid modifying shared response (race).
evidence: diff adds decodeRegionMetaKeyWithShallowCopy and uses it on RegionEpochNotMatch to shallow-copy Region before decoding keys (store/tikv/pd_codec.go, store/tikv/region_cache.go).
